questions.text: What is the primary purpose of client-side programming technologies?

choices.label: A
choices.text: To respond to requests for searching the Internet

choices.label: B
choices.text: To build applications that run in the user's browser

choices.label: C
choices.text: To manage bank-account balances on a remote server

choices.label: D
choices.text: To store structured sets of data in a database

questions.explanation: Client-side technologies are used to build web pages and applications that run on the client, specifically in the browser on the user's device.

questions.text: Which organizations collaborated to create the HTML5 version of the language?
questions.explanation: HTML5 is a cooperation between the World Wide Web Consortium (W3C) and the Web Hypertext Application Technology Working Group (WHATWG).

questions.text: Which of the following is a key rule established for HTML5 development?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Increase reliance on external plugins like Flash

choices.label: B
choices.text: Reduce the need for external plugins

choices.label: C
choices.text: Keep the development process private from the public

choices.label: D
choices.text: New features should be based on XML only

questions.explanation: One of the fundamental rules of HTML5 is to reduce the need for external plugins like Flash.

questions.text: What does the term "portable" mean in the context of Internet and web programming?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The code can only be used on smartphones

choices.label: B
choices.text: The language is easy to translate to other languages

choices.label: C
choices.text: Applications can run across an enormous range of Internet-enabled devices

choices.label: D
choices.text: The website can be easily downloaded to a USB drive

questions.explanation: Portability allows designing web pages and applications that run across a wide range of Internet-enabled devices, from smartphones to large-screen displays.

questions.text: Which HTML5 element is specifically designed for 2D drawing?
questions.explanation: The `<canvas>` element is one of the new features in HTML5 used for 2D drawing.

questions.text: Which of the following are new content-specific semantic elements in HTML5?
questions.explanation: HTML5 introduced new content-specific semantic elements like `<article>`, `<footer>`, `<header>`, `<nav>`, and `<section>`.

questions.text: The document type declaration (`<!DOCTYPE html>`) is required in HTML5 to:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Ensure the browser renders the page in quirks mode

choices.label: B
choices.text: Specify the version of CSS being used

choices.label: C
choices.text: Ensure the browser renders the page in standards mode

choices.label: D
choices.text: Link the document to a web server

questions.explanation: The DOCTYPE is required so that browsers render the page in standards mode rather than quirks mode.

questions.text: In HTML5, comments are delimited by which of the following?
questions.explanation: HTML5 comments start with `<!--` and end with `-->`.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the `<head>` section in an HTML5 document?

choices.label: A
choices.text: To contain the actual content visible to the user

choices.label: B
choices.text: To contain information about the document, such as title and character set

choices.label: C
choices.text: To define the main footer of the webpage

choices.label: D
choices.text: To store images and videos directly

questions.explanation: The `<head>` section contains information about the document, including the title, character set (UTF-8), and formatting instructions.

questions.text: What is UTF-8?

choices.label: C
choices.text: A variable-width character encoding for Unicode

questions.explanation: UTF-8 is a variable-width encoding that can represent every character in the Unicode character set.

questions.text: Elements that do not have an end tag, such as ``, are called:
questions.explanation: Elements like `` that contain only attributes and cannot have nested content or a closing tag are formally known as void elements in the HTML specification.

questions.text: Where does the text within the `<title>` element typically appear?

choices.label: A
choices.text: In the main body of the webpage

choices.label: B
choices.text: In the browser's title bar or tab

choices.label: C
choices.text: At the very bottom of the document

choices.label: D
choices.text: As the largest heading on the page

questions.explanation: The text within the `<title>` element is displayed in the title bar of the browser window or on the page's tab, and it is also used by search engines.

questions.text: Which heading element represents the most significant heading?
questions.explanation: The `<h1>` element is the highest level of heading and represents the most important heading on the page.

questions.text: What does the `href` attribute in an `<a>` tag specify?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The font style of the link

choices.label: D
choices.text: The name of the user clicking the link

questions.explanation: The `href` (hypertext reference) attribute specifies the URL of the page or resource that the link goes to.

questions.text: Which protocol is used in an anchor tag to link to an email address?
questions.explanation: To create a link that opens the user's email client, the `mailto:` URL scheme is used within the `href` attribute.

questions.text: Why is the `alt` attribute mandatory for the `` element?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It specifies the alternative color of the image

choices.label: B
choices.text: It improves accessibility for visually impaired users using screen readers

choices.label: C
choices.text: It links the image to a high-resolution version

choices.label: D
choices.text: It is required for the image to load faster

questions.explanation: The `alt` attribute provides a text description of the image, which is vital for accessibility (screen readers) and acts as a placeholder if the image fails to load.

questions.text: What is the character entity reference for the ampersand (`&`) symbol?
questions.explanation: The ampersand symbol is a reserved character in HTML and must be represented by the entity reference `&` to be displayed correctly.

questions.text: Which element is used to create an unordered list with bullet points?
questions.explanation: The `<ul>` (unordered list) element defines a list where items are typically marked with bullets, while `<ol>` is for numbered lists.

questions.text: How do you specify the title or caption of an HTML table?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Using the `<title>` tag inside the table

questions.explanation: The `<caption>` element must be the first child of a `<table>` and provides a title or description for the table data.

questions.text: Which attribute allows a table cell to span across multiple columns?
questions.explanation: The `colspan` attribute is used on `<td>` or `<th>` elements to define the number of columns the cell should span.

questions.text: In a form, which `method` appends form data to the URL, making it visible in the address bar?
questions.explanation: The `get` method appends form data directly to the end of the URL, making it visible to the user.

questions.text: What is the function of the `<label>` element in a form?

choices.label: A
choices.text: To submit the form to the server

choices.label: B
choices.text: To provide a multiline text area

choices.label: C
choices.text: To provide users with information about an input element's purpose

choices.label: D
choices.text: To group multiple radio buttons together

questions.explanation: The `<label>` element is used to provide a descriptive label for form inputs, improving usability and accessibility.

questions.text: Which input type allows a user to select only one option from a group of mutually exclusive choices?
questions.explanation: Radio buttons are used when only one option in a group can be selected at a time.

questions.text: How do you link to a specific section of the same document (internal linking)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: By using the `href="#id"` syntax where "id" matches the target element's ID

choices.label: B
choices.text: By using the `<link>` tag with the name of the section

choices.label: C
choices.text: By creating a new `.html` file for every section

choices.label: D
choices.text: Internal linking is not possible in HTML5

questions.explanation: Internal linking is achieved by setting an `id` on the target element and using `#id` as the `href` value in an anchor tag.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the `<meta>` element with the `name="description"` attribute?

choices.label: A
choices.text: To define the character encoding of the page

choices.label: B
choices.text: To provide a list of keywords for search engines

choices.label: C
choices.text: To provide a short summary used by search engines to catalog the site

choices.label: D
choices.text: To specify the author of the document

questions.explanation: The description `<meta>` element provides a short summary of the site, which search engines often display in search results.

questions.text: Which of the following is considered a 'legacy' element that should generally be avoided in favor of CSS for layout and styling?
questions.explanation:
While still part of the HTML5 specification as a thematic break, the `
` (horizontal rule) and `
` (line break) elements were traditionally used for presentational formatting, which is now better handled via CSS.

questions.text: Which element is used to insert a multiline text field in a form?
questions.explanation: The `<textarea>` element is used for multiline user input.

questions.text: In a table, which element contains the primary data of the table?
questions.explanation: The `<tbody>` element contains the primary body/data of the table.

questions.text: What does the `<strong>` element signify?

choices.label: A
choices.text: That the text is a link

choices.label: B
choices.text: That the text has high importance and should typically be rendered in bold

choices.label: C
choices.text: That the text is computer code

choices.label: D
choices.text: That the text is a list item

questions.explanation: The `<strong>` element indicates high importance, and browsers usually render it in bold by default.

questions.text: Which attribute of the `<table>` element was historically used to make it more accessible by describing its content to speech devices?
questions.explanation: The `summary` attribute was used in HTML 4.01 to provide a description of the table's structure and purpose for assistive technologies.

questions.text: Which element is used to define an individual row in a table?
questions.explanation: The `<tr>` (table row) element defines a row in an HTML table.

questions.text: What happens if a web server cannot find a requested document?
questions.explanation: If a server cannot locate a requested resource, it returns a 404 (Not Found) status code.

questions.text: To create a button that clears all user input in a form, which `type` attribute should be used for the `<input>` element?
questions.explanation: The `<input type="reset">` element creates a button that resets all form controls to their initial values.

questions.text: What is the primary role of a web server?

choices.label: B
choices.text: To store HTML documents and respond to client requests

choices.label: C
choices.text: To create images for web pages

choices.label: D
choices.text: To physically connect cables to the Internet

questions.explanation: Web servers are computers that store website files and serve them to users (clients) via the HTTP/HTTPS protocols.

questions.text: Which of these is NOT a web browser that supports HTML5 features?
questions.explanation: WordPad is a basic word processing application, not a web browser. Modern browsers like Chrome, Safari, and Opera provide extensive support for HTML5.

questions.text: What is the significance of 'nesting' in HTML?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It refers to placing one element inside another

choices.label: B
choices.text: It refers to the loading speed of the website

choices.label: C
choices.text: It refers to deleting old or deprecated code

choices.label: D
choices.text: It refers to connecting two different domain names

questions.explanation: Nesting occurs when HTML elements are contained within other elements, maintaining a parent-child hierarchy (e.g., placing `<title>` within `<head>`).

questions.text: Which element provides a drop-down list of items in a form?
questions.explanation: The `<select>` element is used to create a drop-down list, containing several `<option>` elements for the user to choose from.

questions.text: What does the `action` attribute of a `<form>` element define?

choices.label: B
choices.text: The URL to which the form data will be sent

choices.label: C
choices.text: The background color of the form

choices.label: D
choices.text: The visual style of the buttons used in the form

questions.explanation: The `action` attribute specifies the destination URL or script that will process the form data upon submission.

questions.text: Which of the following is a form control introduced in HTML5?
questions.explanation: HTML5 introduced several new input types, including `date` (which renders a calendar picker), `email`, `url`, and `number`.

questions.text: When using the `post` method in a form, where is the data placed?

choices.label: A
choices.text: At the end of the URL

choices.label: C
choices.text: Inside the body of the HTTP request

questions.explanation: Unlike the `get` method, which appends data to the URL, the `post` method sends form data within the body of the HTTP request, making it more secure for sensitive information.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the `<input type="hidden">` element in HTML forms?

choices.label: A
choices.text: To hide the entire form from the user

choices.label: B
choices.text: To store data that should not be visible to the user but is sent to the server when the form is submitted

choices.label: C
choices.text: To encrypt the data being sent

choices.label: D
choices.text: To prevent the user from clicking the submit button

questions.explanation: Hidden inputs are non-visual components used to store data, such as tracking IDs or session keys, that need to be processed by the server without user interaction.

questions.text: Which tag is used for the largest heading and which for the smallest heading in HTML5?
questions.explanation: HTML5 provides six levels of heading elements, `<h1>` through `<h6>`, where `<h1>` represents the highest rank (largest) and `<h6>` the lowest rank (smallest).

questions.text: The `<hr>` tag is used to render a:
questions.explanation: The `<hr>` tag (horizontal rule) is used to represent a thematic break between paragraph-level elements, typically rendered as a horizontal line.

questions.text: What is the function of the W3C Validation Service?

choices.label: A
choices.text: To host your website for free

choices.label: B
choices.text: To ensure an HTML5 document is syntactically correct according to official standards

choices.label: C
choices.text: To convert HTML documents to PDF format

choices.label: D
choices.text: To check the speed of the user's internet connection

questions.explanation: Validation services like W3C's check the markup validity of Web documents in HTML, XHTML, etc., to help ensure cross-browser compatibility and correct rendering.

questions.text: Which image formats are widely considered the most popular for standard web development?
questions.explanation: While several formats are used, PNG (Portable Network Graphics) and JPEG (Joint Photographic Experts Group) are the most common standards for photos and graphics on the web.

questions.text: In a table, the `<tfoot>` section appears where in the final page display?

choices.label: A
choices.text: At the top, above the header

choices.label: B
choices.text: In the middle of the table body

choices.label: C
choices.text: At the bottom of the table

choices.label: D
choices.text: It remains hidden and does not display on the page

questions.explanation: Regardless of its placement in the HTML code (historically placed before the `<tbody>` for performance), the browser renders the `<tfoot>` at the bottom of the table.

questions.text: Which attribute limits the number of characters a user can enter into an `<input>` text field?
questions.explanation: The `maxlength` attribute specifies the maximum number of characters allowed in an `<input>` element.

questions.text: To mask sensitive information like passwords, which input `type` attribute should be used?
questions.explanation: The `type="password"` attribute value defines a password field where characters are masked by asterisks or dots to ensure privacy.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the `<thead>` element in a table?

choices.label: A
choices.text: To store the main data rows

choices.label: B
choices.text: To define the table's header section, typically containing the column labels

choices.label: C
choices.text: To link the table to an external database

choices.label: D
choices.text: To provide a visible caption or title above the table

questions.explanation: The `<thead>` element is used to group the header content in an HTML table, often containing one or more rows of `<th>` elements.

questions.text: What does the abbreviation XHTML stand for?
questions.explanation: XHTML (EXtensible HyperText Markup Language) is a version of HTML that follows the stricter syntax rules of XML.
